{"society_power": 6.5, "state_power": 7.5, "society_change": 0.0, "state_change": 0.0, "key_event": "Ley Maldita bans Communists as labor keeps organizing (CUT)", "reasoning": "The \"Ley Maldita\" and Klein-Saks repression pushed society down, but women's suffrage, urbanization, and the founding of CUT pushed it up, leaving net society power steady; the state gained coercive and developmentalist (CORFO) reach but was undermined by chronic inflation and copper-revenue dependence, netting roughly flat."}
